heroHeadline: DOJ Drops Powell Probe, Warsh Confirmation Path Clears; Super Week Begins Apr 28: FOMC, Q1 GDP, March PCE, ECB All Due
heroSubtitle: Apr 25: The DOJ dropped its criminal investigation of Fed Chair Powell on April 24, removing the final hurdle Sen. Tillis (R-NC) had placed on Kevin Warsh's Senate Banking Committee confirmation vote — the vote is now expected the week of April 28. Powell's term expires May 15 (20 days away). Oil eased to WTI $94.40/Brent $105.33 on April 25 (-3% from April 24 peaks) as Iran-Pakistan-Oman diplomatic back-channels continued. S&P 500 touched an all-time high of ~7,147 this week. 'Super Week' begins April 28: BOJ (April 27, hold expected), FOMC April 28-29 hold decision (Powell's last scheduled meeting as Chair; 94% probability hold at 3.50-3.75%), Conference Board Consumer Confidence (April 29), Q1 2026 GDP advance estimate (April 30; GDPNow 1.24%, consensus 1.5-2.0%), March 2026 PCE inflation (April 30; expected re-acceleration above 3.0%), and ECB policy decision (April 29-30, hold expected). Goldman Sachs warns K-shaped economic effects will 'really bite' in 2026. Recession probabilities: Goldman Sachs 30%, JPMorgan 35%, Moody's Analytics 49%.
footerNote: Data sourced from IMF WEO Apr 2026, IEA Oil Market Report Apr 2026, University of Michigan Surveys of Consumers, US Census Bureau, S&P Global Flash PMI Apr 2026, BIS, Federal Reserve FOMC, ECB Governing Council, Bank of England, BEA, BLS, World Bank, Tax Foundation, Penn Wharton Budget Model, Conference Board, CBOE, Goldman Sachs Research, JPMorgan Global Markets Strategy, Moody's Analytics, EIA, China NBS, CBP CAPE Portal, CNN Business, Bloomberg, CNBC, The National News, Al Jazeera, NPR, NBC News, 24/7 Wall St, OneIndia, Fortune.
